response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of experience, but is critically misaligned with this role on nearly every hard requirement. The position mandates native-level Japanese proficiency — a non-negotiable prerequisite that the candidate does not possess (his languages are English, Romanian, and basic German). Beyond that, his background is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js web development with no evidence of LAN-switch firmware work, Linux kernel modification, or any of the required network security protocol expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). His C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief 2014–2015 role at National Instruments and a university thesis project, far below the depth required for embedded UNIX development at this seniority level.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and systems domains demonstrates broad technical adaptability
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and caching) shows performance-oriented engineering instincts
Some foundational C/C++ and Linux exposure from National Instruments and an academic wave-field synthesis project using Linux and Jack audio
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement; this alone disqualifies the candidate regardless of technical merit
No experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF, switch chip SDKs, or bootloaders)
Linux kernel investigation and source-code modification capability is entirely absent; Linux usage is confined to general development environments and container tooling

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
